title: गोपी गीत
devanagariText:
जयति तेऽधिकं जन्मना व्रजः श्रयत इन्दिरा शश्वदत्र हि।
दयित दृश्यतां दिक्षु तावकास्त्वयि धृतासवस्त्वां विचिन्वते॥१॥

शरदुदाशये साधुजातसत्-सरसिजोदरश्रीमुषा दृशा।
सुरतनाथ तेऽशुल्कदासिका वरद निघ्नतो नेह किं वधः॥२॥

विषजलाप्ययाद्व्यालराक्षसाद्वर्षमारुताद्वैद्युतानलात्।
वृषमयात्मजाद्विश्वतोभयादृषभ ते वयं रक्षिता मुहुः॥३॥

न खलु गोपिकानन्दनो भवानखिलदेहिनामन्तरात्मदृक्।
विखनसार्थितो विश्वगुप्तये सख उदेयिवान् सात्वतां कुले॥४॥

विरचिताभयं वृष्णिधुर्य ते चरणमीयुषां संसृतेर्भयात्।
करसरोरुहं कान्त कामदं शिरसि धेहि नः श्रीकरग्रहम्॥५॥

व्रजजनार्तिहन् वीर योषितां निजपरस्परस्पर्धनं च यत्।
सजल जलदश्रेणिशोभया महति सिञ्च नश्चित्रतापवत्॥६॥

प्रणतदेहिनां पापकर्शनं तृणचरानुगं श्रीनिकेतनम्।
फणिफणार्पितं ते पदाम्बुजं कृणु कुचेषु नः कृन्धि हृच्छयम्॥७॥

मधुरया गिरा वल्गुवाक्यया बुधमनोज्ञया पुष्करेक्षण।
विधिकरीरिमा वीर मुह्यतीरधरसीधुनाप्यायय स्वकाः॥८॥

तव कथामृतं तप्तजीवनं कविभिरीडितं कल्मषापहम्।
श्रवणमङ्गलं श्रीमदाततं भुवि गृणन्ति ते भूरिदा जनाः॥९॥

प्रहसितं प्रिय प्रेमवीक्षणं विहरणं च ते ध्यानमङ्गलम्।
रहसि संविदो या हृदिस्पृशः कुहक नो मनः क्षोभयन्ति हि॥१०॥

चलसि यद्व्रजाच्चारयन्पशून् नलिनसुन्दरं नाथ ते पदम्।
शिलतृणाङ्कुरैः सीदतीति नः कलिलतां मनः कान्त गच्छति॥११॥

दिनपरिक्षये नीलकुन्तलैर्वनरुहाननं बिभ्रदावृतम्।
घनरजस्वलं दर्शयन् मुहुर्मनसि नः स्मरं वीर यच्छसि॥१२॥

प्रणतकामदं पद्मजार्चितं धरणिमण्डनं ध्येयमापदि।
चरणपङ्कजं शन्तमं च ते रमण नः स्तनेष्वर्पयाधिहन्॥१३॥

सुरतवर्धनं शोकनाशनं स्वरितवेणुना सुष्ठु चुम्बितम्।
इतररागविस्मारणं नृणां वितर वीर नस्तेऽधरामृतम्॥१४॥

अटति यद्भवानह्नि काननं त्रुटिर्युगायते त्वामपश्यताम्।
कुटिलकुन्तलं श्रीमुखं च ते जड उदीक्षतां पक्ष्मकृद्दृशाम्॥१५॥

पतिसुतान्वयभ्रातृबान्धवानतिविलङ्घ्य तेऽन्त्यच्युतागताः।
गतिविदस्तवोद्गीतमोहिताः कितव योषितः कस्त्यजेन्निशि॥१६॥

रहसि संविदं हृच्छयोदयं प्रहसिताननं प्रेमवीक्षणम्।
बृहदुरः श्रियो वीक्ष्य धाम ते मुहुरतिस्पृहा मुह्यते मनः॥१७॥

व्रजवनौकसां व्यक्तिरङ्ग ते वृजिनहन्त्र्यलं विश्वमङ्गलम्।
त्यज मनाक् च नस्त्वत्स्पृहात्मनां स्वजनहृद्रुजां यन्निषूदनम्॥१८॥

यत्ते सुजातचरणाम्बुरुहं स्तनेषु
भीताः शनैः प्रिय दधीमहि कर्कशेषु।
तेनाटवीमटसि तद्व्यथते न किंस्वित्
कूर्पादिभिर्भ्रमति धीर्भवदायुषां नः॥१९॥
transliteration:
Jayati Te-Adhikam Janmana Vrajah Shrayata Indira Shashvad-Atra Hi.
Dayita Drishyatam Dikshu Tavakas-Tvayi Dhrita-Asavas-Tvam Vichinvate.||1||

Sharad-Udashaye Sadhu-Jata-Sat-Sarasijodara-Shrimusha Drisha.
Surata-Natha Te-Ashulka-Dasika Varada Nighnato Neha Kim Vadhah.||2||

Visha-Jala-Apyayad-Vyala-Rakshasad-Varsha-Marutad-Vaidyuta-Analat.
Vrisha-Mayat-Majad-Vishvato-Bhayad-Rishabha Te Vayam Rakshita Muhuh.||3||

Na Khalu Gopika-Nandano Bhavan-Akhila-Dehinam-Antaratma-Drik.
Vikhanasa-Arthito Vishva-Guptaye Sakha Udeyivan Satvatam Kule.||4||

Virachita-Abhayam Vrishni-Dhurya Te Charanam-Iyusham Samsriter-Bhayat.
Kara-Saroruham Kanta Kamadam Shirasi Dhehi Nah Shrikara-Graham.||5||

Vraja-Jana-Arti-Han Vira Yoshitam Nija-Jana-Paraspara-Spardhanam Cha Yat.
Sajala Jalada-Shreni-Shobhaya Mahati Sincha Nash-Chitra-Tapavat.||6||

Pranata-Dehinam Papa-Karshanam Trina-Chara-Anugam Shri-Niketanam.
Phani-Phana-Arpitam Te Padambujam Krinu Kucheshu Nah Krindhi Hric-Chhayam.||7||

Madhuraya Gira Valgu-Vakyaya Budha-Manojnaya Pushkarekshana.
Vidhi-Karir-Ima Vira Muhyatir-Adhara-Sidhuna-Apyayaya Svakah.||8||

Tava Kathamritam Tapta-Jivanam Kavibhir-Iditam Kalmashapaham.
Shravana-Mangalam Shrimad-Atatam Bhuvi Grinanti Te Bhurida Janah.||9||

Prahasitam Priya Prema-Vikshanam Viharanam Cha Te Dhyana-Mangalam.
Rahasi Samvido Ya Hridi-Sprishah Kuhaka No Manah Kshobhayanti Hi.||10||

Chalasi Yad-Vrajat-Charayan-Pashun Nalina-Sundaram Natha Te Padam.
Shila-Trina-Ankuraih Sidatiti Nah Kalilatam Manah Kanta Gachchhati.||11||

Dina-Parikshaye Nila-Kuntalair-Vanaruha-Ananam Bibhrad-Avritam.
Ghana-Rajasvalam Darshayan Muhur-Manasi Nah Smaram Vira Yachchhasi.||12||

Pranata-Kamadam Padmaja-Architam Dharani-Mandanam Dhyeyam-Apadi.
Charana-Pankajam Shantamam Cha Te Ramana Nah Staneshu-Arpa-Yadhihan.||13||

Surata-Vardhanam Shoka-Nashanam Svarita-Venuna Sushthu Chumbitam.
Itara-Raga-Vismaranam Nrinam Vitara Vira Nas-Te-Adhara-Amritam.||14||

Atati Yad-Bhavan-Ahni Kananam Trutir-Yugayate Tvam-Apashyatam.
Kutila-Kuntalam Shri-Mukham Cha Te Jada Udikshtam Pakshma-Krid-Drisham.||15||

Pati-Suta-Anvaya-Bhratri-Bandhavan-Ativilanghya Te-Antya-Achyuta-Agatah.
Gati-Vidas-Tava-Udgita-Mohitah Kitava Yoshitah Kas-Tyajen-Nishi.||16||

Rahasi Samvidam Hric-Chhaya-Udayam Prahasita-Ananam Prema-Vikshanam.
Brihad-Urah Shriyo Vikshya Dhama Te Muhur-Ati-Spriha Muhyate Manah.||17||

Vraja-Vana-Okasam Vyaktir-Anga Te Vrijina-Hantry-Alam Vishva-Mangalam.
Tyaja Manak Cha Nas-Tvat-Spriha-Atmanam Svajana-Hrid-Rujam Yan-Nishudanam.||18||

Yat-Te Sujata-Charana-Amburuham Staneshu
Bhitah Shanai Priya Dadhimahi Karkasheshu.
Tena-Atavim-Atasi Tad-Vyathate Na Kinsvit
Kurpadibhir-Bhramati Dhir-Bhavad-Ayusham Nah.||19||
hindiMeaning:
हे दयित (प्रिय)! आपके जन्म से व्रज की महिमा और बढ़ गई है। यहाँ सदैव लक्ष्मी निवास करती हैं। हे प्रिय! दिशाओं में दिखाई दीजिए। आपमें प्राण धारण करने वाली आपकी दासियाँ आपको खोज रही हैं।

शरद ऋतु के सरोवर में सुंदर खिले हुए कमल के समान आपके नेत्रों की शोभा से हमारी शोभा चुरा ली गई है। हे सुरत-नाथ! हे वरद! हम आपकी अमूल्य दासियाँ हैं। क्या हमें मारना शोभा देता है?

विषैले जल से, सर्प राक्षस से, वर्षा और आँधी से, बिजली और अग्नि से, वृषभासुर से - सभी प्रकार के भय से, हे श्रेष्ठ! आपने हमारी बार-बार रक्षा की है।

आपकी कथा का अमृत तप्त जीवन को शीतलता देने वाला है, कवियों द्वारा गाया गया है, पापों को नाश करने वाला है, सुनने में मंगलकारी है - ऐसा कथामृत पृथ्वी पर बहुत कम लोग गाते हैं।
benefits: Awakens the deepest form of divine love (prema-bhakti)
Purifies the heart and removes material attachments
Considered one of the most exalted prayers in Bhagavata tradition
Invokes the nectar of krishna-katha to the listener
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and bestows spiritual auspiciousness
Deepens the devotee's longing for the Supreme
description: Gopi Geet — गोपी गीत — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Krishna. This sacred composition is sourced from Bhagavata Purana (Canto 10, Chapter 31) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to deepen the devotee's longing for the Supreme as well as purify the heart and remove material attachments. Additionally, it is known to awaken the deepest form of divine love (prema-bhakti). Recitation is particularly auspicious during Janmashtami. At 19 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 15 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Gopi Geet in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Gopi Geet from Bhagavata Purana in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. The song of the Gopis expressing divine love for Lord Krishna.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gopi Geet?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopi Geet (गोपी गीत — literally Song of the Gopis) is the celebrated 19-verse Sanskrit lament sung by the cowherd maidens of Vrindavana when Sri Krishna suddenly disappeared from the Maha-Rasa, the great circle dance on the autumn full moon night of Sharad Purnima. The hymn is preserved as Chapter 31 of the Tenth Canto (Dashama-skandha) of the Srimad Bhagavata Mahapurana, the supreme scripture of Krishna-bhakti compiled by Veda-Vyasa and narrated to King Parikshit by Suka-deva. When Krishna vanished to humble the gopis' subtle pride at being chosen for the rasa, they wandered the Vrindavana groves searching for him, and finally collapsed at his disappearance-spot to sing this aching nineteen-verse song of viraha-bhakti — combining helpless surrender, divine reproach, philosophical insight, and the supreme declaration that his katha-amrita (story-nectar) is the very life of the suffering. Krishna reappears and dances the unique Sharad Rasa with each gopi simultaneously. (Source: Srimad Bhagavatam 10.31.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Tava Kathamritam — the most famous verse?
seoFaqs.answer: Tava katha-amritam tapta-jivanam, kavibhir-iditam kalmasha-apaham, shravana-mangalam shrimad-atatam, bhuvi grinanti ye bhurida janah (Bhagavata Purana 10.31.9) is universally regarded as the supreme Bhagavata verse on Hari-katha-shravana. Word-by-word: tava katha — the story of You, amritam — is nectar, tapta-jivanam — the very life of those tormented (by samsara); kavibhir-iditam — extolled by the great poet-sages, kalmasha-apaham — destroyer of all sins; shravana-mangalam — auspicious to hear, shrimad-atatam — laden with shri (auspicious wealth), spread far and wide; bhuvi grinanti — those who sing on earth, ye bhurida janah — they are the supreme givers (the most magnanimous of all donors). The Gaudiya, Vallabha, Madhva and Sri-Vaishnava commentators all consider this the locus classicus on Hari-katha-shravana being the surest path of bhakti in Kali-yuga. Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu held this verse above all others in his final years at Puri. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 10.31.9.)

seoFaqs.question: Where does the Gopi Geet appear in the Bhagavata Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopi Geet is preserved as the entire 19-verse Chapter 31 (Ekatrimsho'dhyayah) of the Tenth Canto (Dashama-skandha) of the Srimad Bhagavata Mahapurana — the canto known as the Krishna-charita, devoted exclusively to the leelas of Sri Krishna. The Maha-Rasa narrative spans chapters 29-33 of Skandha 10: Chapter 29 — Krishna calls the gopis with his flute on the autumn full moon (rasa-arambha); Chapter 30 — gopis search for Krishna in the forest after his sudden disappearance, finding the footprints with Radha (gopi-anveshana); Chapter 31 — the Gopi Geet itself, the lament-prayer; Chapter 32 — Krishna reappears and dialogues with them; Chapter 33 — the Maha-Rasa dance proper, where Krishna multiplies himself to dance simultaneously with each gopi. The five chapters together (29-33) are called the Rasa-pancha-adhyayi, the supreme heart of the Bhagavata. (Source: Srimad Bhagavatam Skandha 10, chapters 29-33.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Gopi Geet be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopi Geet is most powerful on Sharad Purnima (Ashvin Shukla Purnima — the autumn full moon night when the Maha-Rasa actually occurred; in Vrindavana this is celebrated as the supreme festival of the Maha-rasa), Janmashtami (Krishna's birth), Radha Ashtami (Radha's birthday), Govardhana Puja, Gopashtami (Kartik Shukla Ashtami), Vaikuntha Ekadashi, all Ekadashis, and on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar). Daily recitation in the morning or evening sandhya, especially during the Chaturmasya months and the Karttika-vrata month, is the standard practice of Gaudiya, Pushtimargi, Nimbarka, Vallabha and Madhva Vaishnavas. The Gopi Geet is also recited at the Maha-Rasa-sthala in Vrindavana on Sharad Purnima, at Govardhana, at the Yamuna ghats, and as a daily reading during Bhagavata Saptaha (week-long Bhagavata recitation). (Sources: Vaishnava sampradaya tradition; Bhagavata Saptaha vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does reciting the Gopi Geet grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Gopi Geet: (1) awakening of viraha-prema-bhakti — the supreme bhakti-rasa of separation, considered higher than even union by Gaudiya theology because separation intensifies remembrance, (2) purification of the heart from material attachments, since the gopis represent the soul's renunciation of all earthly bonds for Krishna alone, (3) the merit of joining the gopis themselves in their direct love of Krishna — sahacharya-bhakti, (4) the kalmasha-apaham promise of verse 9 — destruction of all sins through hari-katha-shravana, (5) preparation for the supreme realisation of Krishna as Atmaram, the inner Self of every being (verse 4: akhila-dehinam-antaratma-drik), (6) admission into the Maha-Rasa of Goloka Vrindavana through bhava-tadatmya identification with the gopis, and (7) liberation through suddha-bhakti as taught by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. (Sources: Bhagavata phala-shruti; Vaishnava acharya commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Gopi Geet and the Venu Geet?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are gopi-songs from the Krishna-charita of the Bhagavata Mahapurana but occur at different stages of the Vrindavana leela and express different bhakti-bhavas. The Venu Geet (Bhagavata 10.21) is the song the gopis sing in spring describing the bewitching effect of Krishna's flute (venu) on all creation — birds, deer, rivers, trees, clouds — when they hear his song from the forest; it is a song of attraction and awakening longing (purva-anuraga). The Gopi Geet (Bhagavata 10.31) is the song the gopis sing in autumn during the Maha-Rasa Sharad Purnima, after Krishna has disappeared from the rasa-mandala to humble their subtle pride; it is a song of viraha — separation-pain — and supreme surrender. Venu Geet evokes the ascending attraction of bhakti; Gopi Geet expresses the depth of viraha-prema. The traditional sequence is: Venu Geet, Yugala Geet (10.35), Bhramara Geet (10.47), Gopi Geet (10.31). All four are central to Vaishnava upasana. (Source: Bhagavata Skandha 10.)
